title: Passenger Service Systems Review: 2026 Best Tools for Improving Passenger Experience
description: In-depth review of top passenger service systems including Amadeus Passenger Service, SITA Passenger Experience, TravelSky Service Platform, comparing self
content:
Passenger Service Digitalization Trends

Passenger experience has become airline and airport competition core. IATA survey shows 2025 passenger airline/airport selection key factors: convenience accounts for 35% (booking convenient, check-in fast, clearance smooth); transparency accounts for 25% (flight information real-time, delay notification timely, baggage tracking visible); personalization accounts for 20% (service recommendations match preferences, special needs met); responsiveness accounts for 20% (complaint processing fast, abnormal service proactive). Passenger service systems are core tools for digitalization improving passenger experience.

Passenger Service Pain Points

Passenger service faces five major challenges: information push lagging, flight delays passengers passively waiting for notification, average lag 30 minutes; limited self-service, self-service check-in and self-service baggage check coverage only 50%-60%, manual counter queue times long; missing personalization, passenger preferences (seats, dining, language) lack recording, service standardized for all passengers; slow feedback response, passenger complaint processing cycle 48 hours+, satisfaction significantly decreased; passive abnormal service, flight cancellations and baggage loss trigger passive service, passenger experience severely damaged.

Passenger Service System Value

According to IATA 2025 Passenger Experience Report, airlines/airports deploying professional passenger service systems achieve on average: passenger satisfaction improvement of 15-20 percentage points; self-service coverage from 50% to 80%; information push timeliness from 30 minutes shortened to 5 minutes; complaint processing cycle from 48 hours to 12 hours; abnormal service proactivity from passive waiting to active push. Passenger service systems are experience competition key weapons.

Top Passenger Service Systems Review

Amadeus Passenger Service Platform

PSS Integration Advantage

Amadeus Passenger Service Platform native integrated with Altéa PSS, passenger data real-time accurate, service personalization high. Serving Lufthansa, Air France-KLM, Iberia and other European airlines, as well as Singapore Changi Airport, Frankfurt Airport and other hub airports.

Core Functional Modules

Self-service Full Process: Self-service check-in (Web/App/Airport kiosk) coverage 90%, self-service seat selection, upgrade, and ancillary services purchase. Self-service baggage check integrated, baggage tags automatically printed. Self-service boarding scan, facial recognition boarding. Self-service clearance, biometric verification.

Real-time Information Push: Flight status real-time tracking, delay prediction 2-3 hours advance push. Boarding reminder, boarding gate change, and baggage collection information real-time push. Push channels: App, SMS, email, social media. Push personalized, passenger preference language and format.

Personalized Service Recommendations: Passenger preference recording: seat preference, dining preference, language, and special needs (wheelchair, infant). Service recommendation AI algorithm: dining recommendations, upgrade recommendations, and destination service recommendations. Recommendation accuracy 75%, conversion rate improved.

Passenger Feedback Management: Multi-channel feedback: App, email, social media, and airport counters. Feedback classification: complaints, suggestions, and compliments. Feedback processing process automated, responsibility assignment, progress tracking, and satisfaction follow-up. Complaint processing time shortened by 60%.

Abnormal Service Response: Flight delays/cancellations automatically trigger passenger notification, rebooking options push, and compensation plan suggestions. Baggage loss automatic report, tracking status real-time push, and claims process initiation. Abnormal service proactivity improved.

Technical Architecture

Cloud SaaS, native integrated with Amadeus PSS, DCS, BMS. Mobile app iOS/Android, passenger coverage 85%. Strong API openness, third-party services (hotel, car rental) integration convenient. Real-time data synchronization delay <5 seconds.

Success Cases

Singapore Changi Airport adopting Amadeus passenger service achieved passenger satisfaction from 80% to 92%, self-service check-in rate 85%, and complaint processing time from 48 hours to 8 hours. Investment $5 million, annual passenger service cost savings $3 million.

Pricing Strategy

Annual subscription fee based on passenger throughput: 10-million-level airports/airlines $4 million-$6 million/year 80%, 5-million-level $2 million-$3 million. Implementation costs approximately 60%-80% of annual fees. Suitable for units with existing Amadeus PSS.

SITA Passenger Experience Platform

Aviation-specific Service Solution

SITA Passenger Experience Platform designed specifically for aviation industry, ready-to-use, industry adaptation complete. Serving more than 200 airlines globally and more than 400 airports. Core advantage is airport-airline coordinated service, passenger journey full touchpointconnect.

Functional Highlights

Passenger Journey Management: Pre-booking (marketing reach), pre-travel (check-in push), in-travel (real-time service), and post-travel (satisfaction survey) full journey coverage. Journey touchpoints automated, service timing precise.

Self-service Integration: Self-service check-in, self-service baggage check, self-service boarding scan, and self-service clearance full process support. Self-service coverage 80%. Airport self-service equipment management, equipment status monitoring.

Passenger Notification System: Flight status, boarding reminder, baggage collection, and airport navigation real-time push. Multi-language push, content customizable. Push channels: SMS, email, App, and social media. Push timeliness <5 minutes.

Passenger Feedback Management: Feedback collection: App, email, social media, and airport counters. Feedback analysis: satisfaction scoring, complaint classification, and improvement suggestions extraction. Feedback response: automated process, responsibility assignment, and satisfaction follow-up.

Airport Service Integration: Integrated with airport navigation, commercial services, parking services, and VIP lounge services. Passenger app embedded airport service entry, one-stop service. Airport-airline service coordinated, passenger experience seamless.

Technical Features

Cloud SaaS, integrated with SITA AODB and flight systems. Mobile app supports iOS/Android. API openness moderate, third-party service integration requires customization. Multi-language support complete.

Customer Results

Medium European airport adopting SITA Passenger Experience Platform achieved passenger satisfaction improvement of 18 percentage points, self-service coverage 75%, and complaint processing time shortened by 50%. Investment $2.5 million, annual service cost savings $1.2 million.

Pricing Advantages

Annual subscription fee: Medium airports/airlines $1.5 million-$2.5 million/year 80%, major $3 million-$4 million. Implementation costs approximately 40%-50% of annual fees. Cost-performance leading in passenger service field.

TravelSky Passenger Service Platform

Domestic Service Solution

TravelSky Passenger Service Platform is Chinese airlines/airports localization first choice, deeply integrated with domestic passenger service habits, WeChat ecosystem, and CAAC policy requirements. Serving Air China, China Eastern, China Southern, Beijing Daxing Airport, Guangzhou Baiyun Airport and others.

Localized Functions

WeChat Ecosystem Integration: WeChat mini-program passenger service entry, query booking, check-in processing, baggage tracking, and complaint feedback integrated. WeChat coverage domestic highest, passenger acceptance strong. WeChat Pay integrated, service payment convenient.

Alipay Service Window: Alipay service window embedded passenger service, payment convenient. Alipay coverage domestic leading.

Health Code Verification: Pandemic period health code verification integrated, verification efficiency improved. Normalized health declaration function.

Facial Recognition Boarding: Facial recognition check-in, boarding, and clearance full process support. Biometric coverage domestic leading. Paperless clearance rate 80%.

Passenger Feedback Chinese Localization: Passenger complaints, suggestions, and compliments all Chinese management. Complaint processing process automated, responsibility assignment, progress tracking, and satisfaction follow-up. CAAC service quality assessment data support.

Technical Deployment

Cloud deployment primarily, local deployment optional. Mobile WeChat mini-program domestic acceptance highest. API integration with domestic airline systems and airport systems complete.

Application Cases

Beijing Daxing Airport adopting TravelSky passenger service achieved WeChat service users exceeding 5 million, self-service check-in rate 85%, complaint processing time from 72 hours to 24 hours, and passenger satisfaction improvement of 15 percentage points. Investment RMB 4 million.

Pricing Advantages

Major airlines/airports annual fees RMB 3 million-5 million, medium 1.5 million-2.5 million. Implementation costs approximately 30%-40% of annual fees. Domestic product cost-performance significantly better than international brands.

Collins Aerospace Passenger Service

Onboard Service Professional Solution

Collins Aerospace (Raytheon Technologies subsidiary) focuses on onboard passenger service, IFE (in-flight entertainment system), onboard Wi-Fi, and onboard dining management system are its core. Serving Delta Air Lines, United Airlines, Qantas and more than 80 airlines.

Differentiated Functions

Onboard Entertainment Personalization: IFE passenger preference recording, entertainment content recommendations. Multi-language entertainment content, passenger language matching. Onboard entertainment data tracking, content popularity analysis.

Onboard Wi-Fi Management: Onboard Wi-Fi connection, traffic management, and charging model. Passenger Wi-Fi usage recording, preference analysis. Onboard Wi-Fi service quality monitoring.

Onboard Dining Customization: Pre-boarding dining reservation function, passenger preference recording. Onboard dining service tracking, dining satisfaction survey. Special dining (vegetarian, halal) needs met.

Onboard Service Feedback: Onboard service satisfaction real-time survey, service improvement suggestions. Onboard service personnel performance tracking.

Integration and Deployment

Onboard system integrated, connected with airline CRM and passenger preference data. Mobile app pre-boarding dining reservation and entertainment content preview.

Pricing Model

Per aircraft subscription: IFE system $150,000/aircraft one-time + annual maintenance $10,000, onboard Wi-Fi $200,000/aircraft + annual fee $20,000, dining management $30,000/year 80%/airline. Onboard service professional solution, onboard experience improved.

Selection Decision Framework

Requirements Matching Principles

Passenger service system selection needs tos to consider six factors: existing system integration (existing Amadeus PSS prioritize Amadeus Passenger Service); operational scope (international operations choose Amadeus/SITA, domestic market choose TravelSky); onboard service needs (onboard experience focus choose Collins); mobile preference (domestic passengers WeChat acceptance high choose TravelSky); self-service goal (self-service coverage target 80%+ choose Amadeus); budget constraints (high budget Amadeus, medium budget SITA, low budget TravelSky).

Implementation Success Factors

Passenger Habit Survey: Passenger mobile usage habits, language preferences, and service acceptance survey. System design matches passenger habits, acceptance high.

Process Simplification: Self-service process simplified, operation steps reduced. Passenger training support, self-service guidance. Process simplification improves self-service coverage.

Service Personnel Training: Service personnel new system training, service process adjustment adaptation. Training cycle 2-3 months.

Continuous Optimization: Passenger feedback data continuous analysis, system optimization iteration. Establish monthly evaluation and quarterly iteration mechanism.

2026 Passenger Service Trends

Biometric Full Process

In 2026, biometric coverage check-in, security screening, boarding, and clearance full process. Facial recognition becomes standard, passengers no need to present documents. Biometric accuracy breakthrough 99%, clearance efficiency improved by 50%. IATA One ID standard widespread.

Paperless Clearance Widespread

Electronic boarding passes, electronic baggage tags, and electronic visas all digital, passengers no need paper documents. Paperless clearance rate from 60% to 85%. Environmental protection and convenience dual value.

AI Personalized Service

AI algorithms deep learn passenger preferences, personalized recommendations covering flights, cabin classes, dining, and destination services. Recommendation accuracy 85%, conversion rate improved. Personalized service becomes competitive differentiation.

Passenger Journey Visualization

Passenger app real-time presents journey progress: check-in completed, security cleared, boarding status, and baggage tracking. Journey transparency improved, passenger anxiety reduced. Visualization becomes standard function.

Abnormal Service Proactivity

Flight delays, cancellations, and baggage anomalies automatically trigger service push, rebooking options, compensation plans, and calming information proactive push. Abnormal service from passive waiting to proactive care, passenger satisfaction improved.

Summary Recommendations

Passenger service system selection needs tos to match existing systems, operational scope, passenger habits, and service goals. Amadeus Passenger Service native integrated with PSS, suitable for units with existing Amadeus systems; SITA Passenger Experience ready-to-use, suitable for medium airlines/airports; TravelSky Platform localization best, suitable for Chinese airlines/airports; Collins Onboard Service professional, suitable for airlines focusing on onboard experience. Implementation success depends on passenger habit survey, process simplification, service personnel training, and continuous optimization. In 2026, biometric full process, paperless clearance, AI personalization, journey visualization, and abnormal service proactivity five trends will reshape the passenger service landscape.
author: Passenger Experience Research Team
